scope: FQ-15: a schematic map/minimap with discovered-band tracking, hall/player/ore/threat markers limited to scouted bands, persistence, and a scouting hook for exploration perks. No gameplay math change; the only save addition is the compact discovered-band list.
summary: Coheronia now has a map you earn by walking. FQ-15 buckets the world into coarse 16-tile bands (scripts/world/map_state.gd, a pure and testable tracker) and reveals the band around the player as they explore, so the map fills in with movement rather than showing everything at once. game_root reveals bands every frame and, on demand when the panel is open, assembles a snapshot: the Town Hall and player positions always, plus ore pockets and live enemy pressure but only inside bands that have actually been scouted - the map never X-rays undiscovered ground. Discovered bands are tiny to store (about seventy-five at most for a full world), so they persist in the world save via a compact string list (map_revealed) that round-trips through save_manager on load. The panel itself (scripts/ui/map_panel.gd) is a compact centered schematic drawn straight from the snapshot - an unseen field, lit revealed bands, and square markers for the hall, the player, ore, and threats - built hidden in the HUD and toggled with M, refreshed a few times a second while open. Crucially the run also lands the scouting hook the spec asked for: _scout_reveal_radius reads the explorer lane's existing biome_reveal perk (effect_key map_discovery_speed, 'New regions map themselves faster') and widens the scouted band when it is owned, giving future exploration perks a live, uniform place to plug in without inventing new data. Four smoke checks prove a revealed cell marks its 3x3 band and not the far world (with the save form round-tripping), that the snapshot carries every field and real hall/player markers, that discovered bands survive a game_root serialize/apply cycle, and that the panel toggles while the biome_reveal perk widens the reveal radius from one band to two. Suite 302 -> 306. The public docs were fully refreshed - README to 306 with a new scoutable-world highlight and an advanced roadmap, plus HANDOFF, VARIABLE_MATRIX, and FABLE_TASK_QUEUE - and the operator's separate in-progress block-art pipeline was left untouched and unstaged.
acceptance: Player can open a map panel (toggle_map / M; hud map panel)
Town Hall and player position are visible (map_snapshot markers; fq15_map_snapshot_markers, hall at (120,29))
Discovered state persists - the compact band list round-trips through the world save (fq15_map_persists)
Discovered surface/cave bands tracked; revealing marks the local band, not the far world (fq15_reveal_bands)
Ore pockets and enemy pressure marked only inside scouted bands
Scouting hook for future perks: the explorer biome_reveal perk widens the scout radius 1 -> 2 (fq15_map_toggle_and_scout_hook)
Suite 306/306; validator and capsule doctor green
validation: COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited headless run -> 306/306 PASS, 4 new fq15_* checks green
git diff --check -> 0 whitespace errors (LF->CRLF notices only)
decisions: Track discovery in coarse 16-tile bands so the map is reveal-on-explore and the save stays tiny (persisted, not transient)
Limit ore/threat markers to revealed bands so the map never X-rays undiscovered ground
Keep the map model pure and scene-free (fed a snapshot) for testability; compute the snapshot on demand while the panel is open
Use the existing explorer biome_reveal perk (map_discovery_speed) as the live scouting hook rather than inventing new perk data
Persist the discovered-band list through save_manager (map_revealed) alongside the other world state
Leave the operator's separate in-progress block-art work unstaged; commit only the FQ-15 files
Self-review only (no agent spawned); logic covered by 4 smoke checks
risks: Ore markers scan revealed bands on demand; a fully-revealed large world scans many cells when the panel opens (bounded to ~75 bands, acceptable)
The map panel is a fixed-size schematic and is not exercised as a live widget by the headless smoke (data/toggle/snapshot path is covered)
The scouting hook currently only widens the reveal radius by one band; richer exploration perks are future work
next_action: No strict queue head remains; big-ticket playability items (pause/settings/keybinds, save-slot management, build-preview tint, quest/contracts layer, subject/NPC labor MVP).
